title: marketing — trait composition
description: Three owned traits. `BelongsToMarketingEvent` is the FK trait for downstream models (MarketingDelivery, MarketingDeadLetter). `HasMarketingDeliveries` / `HasMarketingDeadLetters` are inverse-side conveniences on the MarketingEvent model, exposing scoped query helpers (->activeDeliveries(), ->latestFailedDelivery(), ->unresolvedDeadLetters()). Order matters when composed with BelongsToTenant — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `marketing_event_id` FK. Adds the `marketingEvent()` belongsTo relation. Only used by MarketingDelivery + MarketingDeadLetter — no downstream module currently attaches to marketing events. Provides a `resolveInEvent(?MarketingEvent)` scope for cross-cutting queries.
usedBy: marketing::MarketingDelivery (required marketing_event_id — every delivery anchored to exactly one event)
marketing::MarketingDeadLetter (required marketing_event_id — every dead letter anchored to exactly one event)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToMarketingEvent` in the trait list so the tenant scope resolves first.
Refuses to save a row whose marketing_event_id points at an event in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling).

owned.description: Composed onto the MarketingEvent model itself. Adds `->deliveries()` (hasMany over MarketingDelivery), `->activeDeliveries()` (scope filter status IN ('pending', 'dispatching')), `->succeededDeliveries()`, `->failedDeliveries()`, `->latestDelivery($provider)` (returns the most recent attempt against a provider), `->latestFailedDelivery($provider)` (used by RetryFailedDeliveriesJob to compute next_retry_at). Also adds `->hasSucceededOn($provider)` boolean helper.

Composed onto the MarketingEvent model itself. Adds `->deadLetters()` (hasMany over MarketingDeadLetter), `->unresolvedDeadLetters()` (scope filter resolved_at IS NULL), `->deadLetterFor($provider)` (returns the dead letter for a specific provider or null). Distinct from HasMarketingDeliveries because dead letters have different lifecycle + retention (indefinite vs 2-year).

framework.reason: MarketingEvent, MarketingProviderConfig. NOT MarketingDelivery (per-attempt audit rows — hard-purged by retention pipeline). NOT MarketingDeadLetter (indefinite retention — hard-deleted only on tenant erasure).

foundation.reason: MarketingEvent (searchable by deduplication_key, subject_id, utm_campaign). NOT the other three (they're accessed via relations from MarketingEvent).

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

audit.reason: Every write to marketing_events / marketing_deliveries / marketing_provider_configs / marketing_dead_letters is compliance-relevant (financial retention 7 years).

activity.reason: MarketingProviderConfig + MarketingDeadLetter — admin actions feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT MarketingEvent + MarketingDelivery — high-volume; already covered by the audit trail.

marketing_event.notes: Order matters: BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. HasMarketingDeliveries + HasMarketingDeadLetters are inverse-side conveniences. NO HasActivityLog — event capture is high-volume + already audited.

marketing_delivery.notes: NO SoftDeletes — deliveries are per-attempt audit rows; hard-purged by PurgeExpiredMarketingDataJob only. NO HasActivityLog — high-volume.

marketing_provider_config.notes: SoftDeletes for the deactivate path (soft-delete + 90-day grace before hard-purge). Auditable is critical — credentials + circuit-breaker state changes are compliance-relevant.

marketing_dead_letter.notes: NO SoftDeletes — dead letters retain indefinitely (financial audit). Auditable + HasActivityLog because every replay/resolve is an ops decision worth surfacing in the activity feed.
